news.title: Haryana clears policy for mixed land use, paves way for pending projects
news.para: Haryana's cabinet has approved a new policy to regulate mixed land use zones, resolving pending project approvals. Residential, commercial, and institutional activities will be permitted without fixed percentage caps, while industrial expansion is restricted. Projects must adhere to a 70:30 ratio for primary to allied uses.

news.title: Workday leases 1.94 lakh sq ft space in Chennai to set up GCC
news.para: Enterprise cloud application company Workday has leased 1.94 lakh sq ft in Chennai's Millenia Business Park, marking a significant commitment to establish a global capability center. The lease, structured in two phases with different commercial models, signals a substantial operational hub for the company in India, with potential for future expansion.

news.title: Collector rates revised; property in Chandigarh to become costlier
news.para: Chandigarh property buyers will face increased costs from April 1 as the administration has raised collector rates across all categories. Residential properties will see the sharpest hikes, with plotted segments up by 33% and housing board flats by 25%. This revision follows a new procedure for calculating rates, with public comments invited before final notification.

news.title: WeWork India to invest ₹31 crore to add 81,166 sq ft space in Bengaluru
news.para: WeWork India has leased additional space in Bengaluru, set to add over 1,200 desks by May 2026. The company plans to invest approximately ₹31 crore in the project, to be funded through internal accruals and debt. Further expansion is happening in Pune, with new desks to be operational by November 2026.

news.title: NCLAT declines interim stay on Adani's ₹14,535-crore bid for Jaiprakash Associates
news.para: The Insolvency Appellate Tribunal has declined to place an interim hold on Vedanta Group's dispute against the NCLT's endorsement of Adani Group's bid for Jaiprakash Associates Ltd. The tribunal has requested a response from the Committee of Creditors within a week, with a further hearing slated for April 10.

news.title: Haryana revises allotment rates for apartment units under affordable group housing projects
news.para: Haryana's Cabinet has approved changes to its Affordable Housing Policy. Allotment rates for apartment units in affordable group housing projects will rise by 10 to 12 percent. This decision aims to encourage developers facing increased costs for land and materials. New rates will apply to licenses yet to make allotments.

news.title: Over 16,000 old properties in Nashik go for redevelopment in seven years
news.para: Nashik has witnessed a significant surge in property redevelopment over the past seven years, with over 16,000 old structures demolished and rebuilt. This trend is driven by ageing infrastructure, safety concerns, and liberalized FSI norms introduced in 2020, encouraging developers to replace outdated buildings with modern, amenity-rich housing.

news.title: Mumbai: Former bank manager, nine others convicted in 2004 home loan fraud
news.para: A Mumbai court has found a retired bank manager and nine accomplices guilty in a 2004 loan fraud scandal. The ringleaders of the scheme were handed down five-year prison sentences, while the former bank employee received a lighter sentence of one year for neglecting essential inspections.

news.title: UK: Bellway cuts margin outlook, echoes sector warnings on Iran war risks
news.para: British homebuilder Bellway has lowered its profit margin forecast. The company is using incentives to attract buyers, which is impacting profitability. Bellway also highlighted market risks from the Middle East conflict. Shares of the company saw a significant drop. Builders are facing a year of margin pressure. Rising building costs and interest rate concerns are affecting demand for new homes.

news.title: UP-RERA to open April 1-15 window for QPR filings, warns of action for non-compliance
news.para: UP-RERA will open its portal for developers to submit quarterly progress reports from April 1 to April 15, 2026. This is mandatory for all registered projects. Developers must update project details within this period. Failure to submit these reports can lead to penalties. This ensures transparency for homebuyers. Recent actions show UP-RERA is enforcing these rules.

news.title: Haryana RERA directs Imperia Structures to pay ₹55 lakh compensation to homebuyers
news.para: Haryana RERA has ordered Imperia Structures to pay homebuyers ₹55 lakh for lost property appreciation. The builder must also compensate for mental agony and litigation costs. Homebuyers booked an apartment in Gurugram's 'The Esfera' project in 2013. Possession was promised by May 2017 but was delayed. The authority previously ordered a refund of ₹73.37 lakh.

news.title: India’s flex office stock crosses 100 million sq ft, demand driven by IT, GCCs: Report
news.para: Crossing the 100 million sq ft mark, India's flexible office space has witnessed remarkable expansion in just five years. At the helm is Bengaluru, boasting the highest concentration of flexible work environments. The surge in demand is primarily fueled by IT, technology, and BFSI industries, with international corporations taking the lead in space absorption.

news.title: ASK Property Fund exits ₹650 crore investment in Mahagun’s Noida project
news.para: ASK Property Fund has successfully exited its investment in a Mahagun Group residential project in Noida. The fund generated a 21% IRR and a 2.1x multiple from this deal. Additionally, ASK Property Fund completed a full exit of its 2018 debt fund, achieving an investment multiple of 1.83x.

news.title: Telangana HC pulls up HYDRAA for taking over plot in Hayathnagar
news.para: The Hyderabad High Court has again criticized HYDRAA for allegedly seizing a 650 square yard plot, deeming the action unconstitutional. Justice NV Shravan Kumar expressed shock at the agency's interference in sub judice property disputes, noting a lack of change in HYDRAA's attitude despite previous court orders. The court has sought a response from HYDRAA by March 25.

news.title: Uttar Pradesh: UPSIDA approves eight new warehousing, logistics projects
news.para: Uttar Pradesh State Industrial Development Authority has approved eight new projects in warehousing and logistics. These projects represent an investment of around Rs 800 crore. The approvals are part of the Uttar Pradesh Warehousing and Logistics Policy-2022. The new projects include silos, warehousing units, and a logistics project. They will be developed across several districts.

news.title: Hyderabad civic body seals Katriya Hotel and Towers over ₹6 crore property tax dues
news.para: Greater Hyderabad Municipal Corporation (GHMC) has sealed Katriya Hotel and Towers for outstanding property tax dues of Rs 6.16 crore, defaulting since 2017. This action is part of a broader enforcement drive against major defaulters, with five other properties also sealed. GHMC urges prompt payment, highlighting a 90% interest waiver.

news.title: Pune: PCMC earns ₹80 crore in property tax through amnesty scheme, likely to miss fiscal target
news.para: Pimpri Chinchwad Municipal Corporation's property tax amnesty scheme, offering a 90% penalty waiver, has seen a strong response, collecting nearly Rs 80 crore in its initial 20 days. Despite this boost, overall collections are projected to fall short of the Rs 1,000 crore target and may not match last year's figures.

news.title: Embassy Developments’ subsidiary faces KIADB order to surrender 78-acre Bengaluru land
news.para: Embassy East Business Park has been ordered by the Karnataka Industrial Areas Development Board to surrender 78 acres of land in Bengaluru within 30 days. KIADB alleges breach of agreement terms, citing unauthorized sub-lease arrangements. EEBP denies the allegations, stating it obtained necessary no-objection certificates and plans to pursue legal remedies.
